history.text: As its location is 195 km north of the Arctic Circle, summer hikers enjoy the midnight sun, while winter visitors may find the light pollution-free location ideal for viewing the aurora borealis.
Abisko (Swedish: ; Northern Sami: Ábeskovvu) is a village in Sápmi (Lapland), in northern Sweden, roughly 200 km north of the Arctic Circle, and near Abisko National Park, located 4 km west of the.

Abisko is also located near the 330 square kilometre (130 sq mi) lake Torneträsk. Research station Abisko is home to the Abisko Scientific Research Station, an important centre for research into Arctic ecology and.

history.label: 1980s
history.text: Abisko is also reachable by car via the highway E10 which has linked Kiruna and Narvik since the early 1980s.

history.label: Growth
history.text: Permafrost is common around the village, although this low-altitude permafrost is disappearing because of global warming and increased snowfall. Transportation Daily passenger electric trains run by SJ AB connect Stockholm with the Norwegian city.

history.label: Civic role
history.text: Other local forms of transportation include hiking and dog-sledding in winter.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Abisko Turiststation, run by the Svenska Turistföreningen (STF), houses many visitors to the park and provides lodging, food, and other amenities, and is one of many similar facilities located periodically along the Kungsleden.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The national park is known for its cross-country skiing opportunities, snowshoeing, and other winter sports (Mount Nuolja and nearby Björkliden provides backcountry skiing and freeriding opportunities).

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The nine-hole golfcourse at Björkliden is the northern most in Sweden.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: One of the most recognized natural sights is Lapporten, a U-shaped valley visible from Abisko.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The research station hosts the Climate Impacts Research Centre, Umeå University, where research, education and outreach on aquatic and terrestrial ecology are undertaken. Climate Abisko has a subarctic climate (Dfc), typical of Northern Sweden.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Summers are cool and rainy with chilly nights and snow covers are often modest except for during milder winters where maritime air brings more precipitation.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Winters are long and cold but somewhat moderated by mild Gulf Stream air coming from the west.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Abisko is a very cloudy village by Swedish standards but is also one of the driest places in the country due to it being in a rain shadow from the Scandinavian Mountains.
